summary:
PROPHETIC VISIONS OF ATTACKS: Brandon recounts visions of various attacks across different locations, including a drone-like flash in New York accompanied by the song "Imagine," and a threat involving black-hooded individuals on Bourbon Street in New Orleans during the Sugar Bowl. He describes seeing large, armored dump trucks and vans filled with fifty-gallon drums of fertilizer, noting a specific warning to remember Timothy McVeigh and a multi-million dollar operation funded from overseas. Additional targets mentioned include churches, Disney World, Las Vegas, and a Red Dawn scenario, alongside a specific vision of three men conducting coordinated stabbing attacks in public areas and metros in Paris.

TRIBULATION AND THE ANTICHRIST: Brandon describes a future period where all children under the age of fifteen are raptured with all the true believers. He details a scenario where individuals are mandated to bring their Bibles to local courthouses, which are then collected and burned by the tens of thousands in places like Jelsma Stadium. Furthermore, he outlines a vision of a historic town becoming a central hub for worshipping the Antichrist, who utilizes a specific green, white, and black flag featuring United Nations imagery and the five pillars of Islam. Citizens dressed in white gowns are required to bow before massive screens on buildings every Friday, and a specific detail involves a Starbucks appearing where an old barbecue restaurant once stood.

SPIRITUAL LAWS OF PROSPERITY: Brandon explains the principles of seedtime and harvest, emphasizing that supernatural wealth transfers require prior financial sowing, tithing, and supporting local churches rather than relying on chance or a spiritual lottery. He shares a personal anecdote about practicing financial obedience by delaying a sacrificial monetary gift meant for 2026 until December 27th, strictly following perceived divine timing rather than giving during Christmas. He stresses that prayers for financial multiplication must be backed by scripture and that individuals must plant specific seeds to harvest specific results, citing a real estate example where land exponentially increased in value.

CHURCH CORRUPTION AND UPCOMING JUDGMENT: Brandon warns against financial and emotional manipulation within large ministries and charismatic circles, recalling a childhood experience where his family suffered after his father donated their only vehicle due to an emotionally charged church service. He shares a prophecy indicating that a major spiritual housecleaning will occur in 2026, leading to the sudden downfall of prominent ministers and television stations who have engaged in corrupt practices behind the scenes. In their place, unfamiliar individuals will rise to prominence over the next three years, prompting Brandon to express a desire to maintain a clean heart and avoid causing harm to others with any given authority.
rawData: [December 28, 2025]-[Praying over new years]-[https://youtu.be/ULb5iK3ua_c]-[Well, good evening family or good morning wherever you are. Thank you for joining us again tonight. I'm going to just pray a little bit and pray over New Year's Eve and whatever they want to talk about. I'll answer some questions, I guess. But we have one little thing that will show you what I believe is a result of prayer. And I think that God is so amazing to me as we see the news before the news comes on. And I want to say this: we give all the glory to Jesus. I want you to know how much I depend on him and how much when people say, 'Bren, you say I a lot.' It's just speech. It's not about me. This is all about him. And everything I do in word and in deed, I give all to the glory of God. I hope you all know that. I can't make this happen; it's all the Holy Spirit. I don't know how else to say it. There's no possible way to do this in the natural. It's all the Holy Spirit. And so it's such an honor that he would even look my direction and want me to even pray and do this on a live stream and pray with y'all and do this YouTube channel like we do. And it's been amazing. We went back and looked at all the things that the Lord has told us to pray over this year. And oh, what a year we've had as we get ready to close out this beautiful year. There's only four days left and I'm just so grateful for every single one of you. There's so many of you that have been so faithful in coming and watching. Some of you showed me what YouTube gives you—what you watch the most at the end of the year—and I was in your top percentage of everything that you watch on YouTube. I thought, 'What an honor.' It's all about Jesus. I know you're not here to watch Brandon; you're here because we pray and I believe we're going to get results. I believe the power of prayer changes things and so do you, or you wouldn't be here either. But that's pretty amazing, huh? So in New Orleans, that was one of the major highlights and we'll go over that. I feel in my spirit to go over what the Lord showed us. He pointed out the night we went right in and I was getting ready to take communion. If anybody remembers watching that video, the moment I was getting ready to take the blood, I went into the spirit and I saw New York again. I saw the flash; it looked like drones. It looked like some kind of a drone attacked. I heard people screaming again. I heard women screaming. It was at the point where I heard the song 'Imagine' playing. I remember in the vision everything was so clear. I could see the flash, but I don't know what would cause something of that magnitude of a flash. As we were praying, then I was taken to New Orleans and the people—it was down Bourbon Street. I could see Bourbon Street again. I could see people with black hoodies on, and it was where the horse and the church in the background was, and where they sell beignets by the river right there. He specifically brought up the Sugar Bowl on New Year's Day to pray over. I believe it's New Year's Day, either the first or the second, but I believe it's the first. Oh, the Sugar Bowl. Yes, the first. Praying over the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. But isn't it interesting that they even bring it up and they're asking for help because they're getting tips? Did he say they caught somebody down there? They said they had a threat. I heard that. It actually sounded like they found somebody. I was trying to follow because that was the first time I'd seen that, and I was like, 'Hey, it looks like they got one.' And so I believe that's the beginning of an unraveling. This is what we're praying over. Can you play that one more time? I just wanted to hear that. I was trying to catch that because I never heard it. I was like, 'Wow.' So they stopped somebody at border patrol, an extremist. So that's kind of interesting. I wonder what it has to do with New Orleans. But you know, I thought, when the Lord spoke to us specifically about semi-trucks running into crowds, it wasn't vans. It was big trucks, like big dump trucks, trying to use a big duty truck—a big, big truck. Not like these little, you know, like a little Hyundai or something. I'm talking about this truck was armored and it was ready. And it goes back to what the Lord showed us. You know, I was praying over the fifty-gallon drum vans with all of the fertilizer, and the Lord kept telling us, 'Remember Timothy McVeigh?' Sorry, I keep itching my nose. I've been outside in the yard praying, and the dirt and stuff makes me itch. I even got dirt all over my fingers because I was playing in the dirt. I love nature. The Lord was reminding me of everything that he showed us this time last year concerning the inauguration when we were praying for the fifty-gallon drums and I saw vans full of fertilizer. And the Lord said, 'Remember Timothy McVeigh.' I truly believe those are not gone. I truly believe the fertilizer and all the things that the Lord showed me, from the detail of the money exchange coming from overseas from a certain country funding this—that this was a multi-million dollar operation that didn't just go away. I saw those vans full of fertilizer, black barrels full of fertilizer. There were huge barrels all in the back, and then I saw dump trucks full of fertilizer. And the Lord said, 'Remember Timothy McVeigh.' So as I was in the yard today, I was still praying and interceding for those dump trucks and those vans. And I'm telling you all that we should be on heightened alert. You should pray. I don't know when, but the Lord said it would be a Red Dawn experience. He keeps bringing those things up to me. Praying over the storm, the enemy within, an infiltration into our homeland security kind of stuff. Praying for our FBI. Praying for our local law enforcement. Praying for our churches because the Lord brought it up. He said, 'Don't forget, Bren.' He brings all these things to me. And Diana, thank God for her taking notes. She keeps all those notes for me and writes all the things down that the Lord tells us—even about casing churches, casing amusement parks, praying over Disney World, praying over Las Vegas when I saw the major truck driving down. And then there was actually a knifing in Paris. Braden told me that, well, we saw that because there was not a lot of access to firearms, they would have knifings. I saw slashers in vans going down the road and jumping out and going into major crowds and just going after people. I saw three men in particular going into a public area in France. So we pray for those things. We saw somebody already with this identical style of attack. I don't know if she made it—the person he cut up. Well, we pray they did. But this is the kind of stuff the Lord's been warning us about, and it was in Paris, the exact place we prayed over. So, did it happen with a van? No. But this is exactly what: 'French police arrest suspect after three stabbings in Paris metro attack.' So he was in a train. He's doing it the same way the Lord told us they were going to. "Where are my children?" They were running through the streets looking for their kids. They had pictures; they had everything in their hands. "Do you see him? Do you know where he is? Have you seen him?" And they were asking everybody, "Have you seen him?" This was going on because all the children from the age of 15 down to newborns were completely gone. I asked the Lord about that. He said that is the age of accountability. Then he said, "Brandon, all the others who don't know me will be left." I saw all of this that I'm showing to you. Go ahead, you can play some of it. I filmed it because the town we lived in at the time is where I was taken to in the future. I want to show you. This is a football field that's right down from the courthouse. Everyone brings their Bibles in because the Antichrist tells everyone they have to bring their Bibles to the courthouses. At the courthouse I showed you down that street, where I was at the beginning of it, they later bring all their Bibles, and there are stacks of Bibles everywhere. The Antichrist forces you to, and then they bring them to this football field right here. It's called Jelsma Stadium. They burn them by the tens of thousands, stacked a story high—a huge fire in the middle of this field. You see that green field? That's going to be one in the future. I saw it burning, full of Bibles. This is a video I made several years ago. It’s a bowl-like stadium, really deep, and it shows how big the stack of Bibles is. It's similar to when Hitler burned all the books and Bibles. You saw it during Indiana Jones, and that’s similar to what I saw when I was standing with Jesus when they burned them. I saw the smoke go up for miles. You can see it from all around. That will come in the future. It's coming, I'm telling you. I'm just letting you all know it's time to get right with Jesus before it's too late. If your heart's not right with Jesus, you need to repent today because a time is coming shortly when you won't have time to repent anymore. It's over. Jesus Christ is coming. I'm just telling you all, I just want to be real with you; it's time to get real with God because Jesus is coming and the rapture of the church is going to take place. Show this again, son. You can show it better from the front. That's the courthouse where they bring all the Bibles in the future. You're mandated by the Antichrist with that flag; he's going to have his thing on there to have that done. You will see that eventually. Here's another building from the Freemasons. This is another building that you will see during the tribulation. They will have this building for the Antichrist and for his people. Watch, it will happen. Just remember I told you who gets left. I'm not trying to be sarcastic; it's just time to be serious. We're running out of time. There's a great revival about to happen on this earth. We're about to see a major outpouring of the Spirit of God and a billion-soul harvest. I'm telling you all, I've been in prayer, interceding for the things that are coming. I want to show you downtown where the people will come. Look at this pan of downtown. All these old brick buildings in this old historic town will be a major hub. I don't know why they pick here. I think it's because of the—do you see this flag? No, go back to the flag after he becomes Antichrist. This is where they worship every Friday at a certain time. He brings in his own flag. I'll try to tell you all what this flag looks like. He puts the five pillars of Islam as stars above the flag. I had my son create this flag out of what I saw when I was in the tribulation with Jesus. It's green, white, and black. They take those sickles and swords and wrap them around the globe for the UN. They take the five pillars of Islam and put them above, and everyone calls him the Mahdi. They worship him on Fridays. Everyone is required to come and praise him at this place right here on Fridays every single week. This is what it looks like. They are all in single-file lines on measured screens on the sides of buildings, and every single person is bowing down and worshipping the Antichrist on screens throughout the states with his flag on the buildings instead of the United States flag. They are all in white gowns. Now, show the building right here; it goes all the way around. This is the major temple where they will worship every Friday. Ladies and gentlemen, I'm telling you, it's coming. It's hundreds of years old, but this is where they will go. They'll have massive screens on the side of this building so you can worship the Antichrist on Friday. If you don't believe in the rapture and you want to be left here, you can come here and worship the Antichrist or you can go get your head cut off because that's what's going to happen. I'm not trying to be crude; I'm being real. Let's turn right here. These are the grounds. There was a massive screen right there, humongous. It points out this way and shows the people going that way. This is a massive building. These are all the grounds. The lines will go back and forth across, and there will be a massive screen on that brown building. One day, there will be a massive screen on that building and the people will worship that way. They come from everywhere around this area to worship him. See, and they have their Scottish Rite signs there, and they come here and have big meetings. See how big all the land is? Now I'm going to show you the courthouse. Remember, when I had the visitation from the Lord, there was no Starbucks in the town. There was an old barbecue house, a Taco Bueno. I told Diana and the kids, "One day that building will be a Starbucks." When I went to the tribulation, there was a Starbucks there. Everybody said, "Brandon, they'll never be able to afford a Starbucks in this town," because it was a little bitty town. But the Lord said there would be a Starbucks there because I saw it. There was lightning all above the Starbucks. Over the years they had a barbecue house there. We're not there yet. I pray for a lot of people, maybe. I don't know. I pray for the supernatural wealth transfer. A lot of people say, 'Brandon, what qualifies this for the wealth transfer?' and I say, 'I believe it's given, and it shall be given unto you. Good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over will men give unto your bosom.' I don't believe people qualify automatically for the wealth transfer unless they're a giver—unless you're sowing and tithing to your church. Why would God be obligated just to give you that if you're not even surrendered unto Him? If you don't tithe and give your first fruits and your offerings to the church, bless your local church, and help your pastor out—if you're stingy with what you have and not even trying to help out—I don't understand why you would even think God's going to give you a creative idea whenever you don't even help with what you have. What qualifies you to think that? 'Well, it's just grace, brother.' That doesn't even line up with spiritual law. The Bible says 30, 60, and 100-fold, but people think they qualify when they don't even have the seed. You don't even have the crop-ability or the harvest-ability to reap that. I always put the seed in the ground, and we always do a sacrificial gift at the beginning of every year. Whatever the Lord lays on my heart as the head of the house to do, I say, 'We're going to give this ahead of time and we're believing God.' This is above the tithe; this is an offering above the offering, and we're going to do whatever we can do, Diana. We're going to pray over it as a family. Every year, I sow for the fruit above the tithe, believing for overflow for the year. I look at what that seed will produce by 30, 60, and 100-fold. If it does that, then I can believe for that number. Say you sowed a certain amount—I'm not good at math, y'all—but say you sowed a hundredfold of $1,000. Once you get that money and you're believing, you have spiritual authority and spiritual law to be able to call that in and ask God to bless it. 'Father, I thank you that you give me creative ideas to bring in that money. Father, You said in Your word that You rebuke the devourer for my sake.' But people are trying to have a spiritual lottery for something they don't even have the seed in the ground for. It's all by what the Bible says. You can't go off what some preacher says on TV who thinks, 'Well, if you sow this, you'll have a miracle.' I believe God is a miracle-working God. I do. But the law of seedtime and harvest is: whatsoever a man sows, that will he also reap. You can't call in a harvest of something you don't have the seed in the ground for. How can a farmer do that? If seedtime and harvest never ceases—whatever the Law of Genesis talks about when it says everything produces after its own kind—what are you sowing? A farmer goes out and puts a half-acre field out there, but he's believing for five acres. That would be real stupid, wouldn't it? 'Well, I sowed it, honey, in that first acre,' but we're going to pray as a family for the whole five acres to come, even though we only planted and sowed for one. That's what a lot of the body of Christ does. I think it's sad. People perish for a lack of knowledge, but everything is produced according to law. How is it multiplication when the Bible says 30, 60, and 100-fold? 'Well, I'm believing for a million-fold.' Come on, man. The Bible doesn't even say a million-fold. The Bible says a thousand-fold: some 30, some 60, and some 100-fold. You have way-out-there La-La Land faith, but if you don't have the seed to believe for that, it all goes by spiritual law. People try to give you all this pie-in-the-sky: 'Well, I'm going to get this, and it's going to happen.' I believe you can sow, and you have a right to call it in on what you sowed. The multiplication comes by what you've sowed. That's what I believe. Now, when you get the harvest of that and you believe God, you keep believing and you sow your $10 or $100—that's consistency; it adds up real quick. But people get off into weird things, and you can't even add spiritual backing behind it. All that you pray, you have to have word-backed prayers. 'If you abide in Me and My words remain in you, you shall ask what you will and it shall be done.' But the Word has to back what you're praying. If you don't have word-backed prayers, it's just like saying, 'Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star.' You're not going to get anything. You must have word-backed prayers. Find scripture that pertains to what you're believing for, but make sure you're practicing what the Word said to do, because you're not going to get what the Word says you could have unless you're doing what the Word said to do. People get flaky, especially in the charismatic circles. You have to be grounded and find the Word to back what you're believing for, but then do what the Word told you to do in the first place. Stay out of sin. Faith works by love; keep love a priority in your house. Take communion. Fellowship with the Father. Sow your seeds and believe for the harvest. Now, where do you sow? You sow into good ground. 'Well, I've been sowing into Brother So-and-So, and I haven't gotten anything for the last five years.' Then stop doing it. Use some wisdom. If the farmer kept planting in that ground, he's going to have to either put some fertilizer in that ground to get it to produce or something. People try to ask me these things all the time, and I think it's just common sense. If I want to sow soybeans, I don't plant corn. If I want a harvest of cotton, or maybe you wanted a really good watermelon, you don't plant cantaloupe. My mind just thinks simply about things, and I see it work. I know what law is, and I understand that a corn plant is only going to produce so much corn. So, you better plant a whole lot more if you want a whole mess of corn. For the measure that you sow, that will you reap. It's even scripture. If you sow a thimbleful, you're going to reap a thimble. If you sow a dump truck, you're going to reap dump trucks. If you sow a vineyard, you're going to reap vineyards. But people make it complicated and think, 'Well, I'm qualified, so XRP is going to make it happen for me.' And I go, 'Okay, but even if it did and you put your money into it, are you going to keep it? Will it be riches without sorrow, or will you lose it all?' 'Well, I won the lottery, brother.' But that's just a game of chance. I'm talking about walking in spiritual authority and law. I don't believe in chance. I don't play the lottery. I don't do scratch-offs. Back in the day, it was fun—every once in a while, I'd go, 'Oh yeah, they got that $5 scratch-off ticket,' and if I won something, I'd take the kids out and get an ice cream—but I don't look at games of chance. I put my hand to something, and whatever I put my hands to, the Lord blesses. I don't look for a mailbox mentality faith. I'm always looking for a miracle in the mailbox. Whatever I put my hands to will prosper. I ask God to bless my hands. I ask God to bless my mind. Show me, Lord, where to do things. And I'll be driving down the road—I kid you not, it's something we talk about all the time—and I'll be praying in the Holy Spirit. He says, "You see that land right there? It just came up for sale." And in the natural, I know I can't buy it. I don't have it. But He's talking to me about things, stretching me, making me believe for more. And in the natural, the money is not there to buy it, but He talks to me about it. He says, "Brandon, that piece of land right there in the next couple of years is going to be worth tens of millions of dollars." I said, "Wow, that's awesome. Praise God. It's worth a million dollars right now." So I look it up and sure enough, a couple of years later, just like He said, a developer bought it. He put houses on it. It's worth tens of millions just like the Lord said. And I look at those things and I grow. It helps me to grow because I realize it helps get that trust level up, because one day He's going to talk and I'm going to have the money and I'm going to buy that land. I'm going to do what He said, and you can take it to the bank. God will bless your hands. Why? Because I practice His presence. I don't look to Him as a genie in the bottle. "Oh God, Holy Ghost, you're like my genie in a little bottle right here. My oil. Tell me what it is. Let me have the lottery numbers, lucky snake eyes 77 or whatever." And you got it. "And Lord, we're going to make it." No, it's flaky. I look at it. I sow the seed. I say, "Father, I spend time with you." And in the least expected times and in unlikely ways, He talks. I'll be outside in the yard pulling weeds—and I don't have weeds right now because it's winter, but I'd still go outside and even move a rock around, just sitting out there. That's what I did today. I sit out there and I listen because He'll talk whenever your mind is off of your problems. Think about how you can pray for somebody else today. Think about how you can maybe pray for your loved ones. Like I was praying for my mom and my dad; they're going through hard times. You listen to people and you take your eyes off of yourself for a minute and pray for somebody else in their need, and God will speak to you and He'll correct you a lot of the time. He'll correct me: "You shouldn't have said that. None of your business. You don't need to say that." And I wasn't even looking for what He said. But He'll talk in the most unlikely way. You'll be like, "Well, look at that. I wasn't even asking for that. But thank you." And I don't just cast it aside. I listen to it and I take note of it. I get my phone out and I use the voice recorder because I look at everything He says as precious. No matter if I was looking for the answer or not, I take those times to go, "Thank you, sir. Thank you, Father. Thank you for taking the time to correct me. For you correct those that you love." And Father, you'll teach me how to make the bigger money when I can handle it. If I'm not able to handle it and it would destroy me and cost me my marriage or cost me my children, then Father, I thank you that you build the character in me. But if I don't have the character to handle it, then Father, I thank you, Lord, that you create in me a clean heart, that I'm able to do what I need to do, and thank you, Lord, that I'm a good steward of what you've given me. And you can stretch this dollar until it looks like a rubber band, or the quarter, or whatever you've got. But I know that if I can sow my offering into good ground, into your church where you're going—and if your church doesn't have good ground, why are you going there? I always check it. Check the ground. Check the pastors. Check it first. Tithe and then sow. But don't dump your load of seed into something until you at least check the ground to see where you're sowing into. And if you start seeing a turnaround and you start seeing increase, then you give a little bit more and you sow a little bit more. But then if you see a cankerworm on it, first check your heart. Check your marriage. Make sure there's no strife, because faith works by love. Keep the main thing the main thing. Keep the relationship vertical. If you go horizontal and look at all this other stuff, it's not always the church's fault. You've got to make sure that you're in the right place at the right time doing exactly what He asked you to do and sowing where He tells you to sow, being obedient. For instance, I was going to sow my seed for my sacrificial gift this year, and I was going to give it back at Christmas time to the minister, and the Lord said, "No." I said, "Why? You don't want me to give it all? I'll give it somewhere else because, Lord, I want to bless your ministry. Let me do this. You don't want me to do that?" He said, "Not yet." I said, "But I'd like to get it in there and celebrate at Christmas." Just celebrating You because I'm giving it to You. I don't give it to the minister; I give it to Jesus because it's His. I don't give it for kudos from the person; I give it to Jesus so He goes, "Well done, Brandon." I don't give it so they'll look and say, "Well, thank you for that extra money, sir. I appreciate that. Oh, good job, Brandon." I give it so Jesus looks at me and says it was the heart. Even if they don't even acknowledge you, I gave it to Him. It was His Christmas; it's His birthday. I sow into the birthday. Let me bless you with a birthday present. Why? The harvest of souls—that's all He cares about. He's the Lord of the harvest. So he said to me, "No, don't give it." And I thought, "Oh no, there must be something wrong. Am I in control or You? When do you want me to give it?" "I want you to do it on the 27th." "On the 27th? Okay." So I told Diana, "We're holding off on that. We're not going to give it at all." She goes, "I don't care, honey. We've already talked about that. We're going to sow. We're believing God for 2026. Let's go and get it in the ground. The faster it's in the ground, the faster the harvest is." I said, "No, the Lord said wait until the 27th." She goes, "Well, I know you hear the Lord." That's interesting. So I waited until I could do it today. And I was so excited about it because I told the Lord, "I'm giving it for my 2026 increase. I'm believing for my 2026 increase. I'm believing for our ministry to not have any lack, not anything, and that whatever you tell us to do to grow our ministry in 2026, we're going to have more than enough." So I sow it. I went today and I got to put it in there. And I felt the anointing all over it. And I pray over it just like we always do. Anoint it with oil, pray over it, take communion, release it by faith. We take it as a family, all laying hands on it, praying over the envelope as we put it in the mailbox to go and deliver it because I want it. David didn't give anything that didn't cost him something. Everything David gave, it cost him something. He even talked about it because they were going to give the sacrifice and he said, "No, no, no. It has to cost me something. I'm only going to give something that cost me something." So it makes your flesh squirm. When it makes your flesh squirm, it's uncomfortable. And that's what we do. And we do it every year. Some years I go, "Oh God, I thank you for this." God loves a cheerful giver. So you help out the people that God tells you to. That's why I always say it's easy for me to come at you and say all this because we never ask for anything. I'm telling you this out of instruction so you can learn. And you say, "Brandon, it's about your ministry." No. I'm not teaching you this to sow to me; I'm teaching this so that you'll learn how to grow in your ministry and what God's called you to do. Do you understand? I hate manipulation, especially in churches and big ministries. There is a lot of manipulation, especially in the Word of Faith circles. I look at it and say you can keep it and use it for whatever the Lord tells you to do. I don't think a preacher should ever be manipulating people. It's wrong, especially using the platform. Follow the Holy Ghost and do what He tells you to do. Mary said, "Whatever He tells you to do, do it." Obey. Fill the water pots with water, and He turned the water into wine. Whatever He tells you to do, do it. Don't listen to the man of God tugging at your heart with emotional violins. "Won't you help me?" No, keep your money. Follow the voice of God for your life. I do not like spiritual manipulation. I've seen it done; I worked for some big ministries. Don't do this. Don't give out of emotion ever. Give when the Holy Spirit tugs at you. Be spirit-led in your giving. Listen to the voice of the Spirit of the Lord concerning this. There are too many high notes on the organ and you say, "I feel the Holy Ghost." No, you didn't feel anything. This isn't the Holy Ghost; it's flesh, and you're being flaky. I'm not going to be giving out of flakiness. I don't care how much you think you need for your orphanage or your project. You want to play a high note on a key on your organ to make me feel like I'm feeling the Holy Ghost, and you play the tambourine. Sit down. It's flaky. You're all excited and people are falling out. It's flakiness, especially in the charismatic circle. Stop it. I'm telling you all, when you're led by the Spirit of God and you listen to Him when He tells you to give, and you're not emotionally being fed some baloney, you're going to reap, and God will bless you. Listen again and check it. Ask your wife or your husband if they feel this way. You may not have a husband or wife, but follow the Holy Ghost. He'll be your guide. Listen to Him and be led by the Spirit of God. I'm so tired of charismatic flakiness. I've seen it all. People giving their last everything. My mom and dad were all hyped up in a service one time and gave our only car away because they needed the building fund paid off. It was our only car, brand new; my uncle bought it for my dad. We were excited to have it because it was a little Cutlass. My dad gave it away because there was a high note on the keyboard, and we suffered. We were freezing in the back of a truck all winter long because we didn't have a car to get to church or school. My dad got a camper from my grandparents, and it was freezing in the negatives. I sat back there with the exhaust fumes, mad at the church because they took my car since Dad got hyped up about someone giving. I've seen it. I've lived it, and it's flaky. Don't give out of emotion; give by the Spirit of God leading you. People want to know my opinion about it, and I tell you, I've seen it all. Some of the biggest people in the biggest ministries have the biggest planes and biggest stuff. I don't care if they have it; I want them to be blessed. But never work on an old lady's emotions to get it. Let it be spirit-led and God will bless your hands. That's my message. There's a mic drop. If you listen to what I'm trying to tell you, you'll walk in a greater side of blessing and you'll have more peace instead of being ticked off at the church because people are getting mad about prosperity. Just stay in the right lane. Did that help at all? Hopefully. I talked too long, but it is what it is. Do you want to pray now? I didn't have anything prepared, but the Lord gave you that. That came right out of the Holy Ghost. I can feel the presence of the Lord on it. He helped me. I just love Him; He's my friend. There is too much flakiness in the church. You've seen it. I know there's a lot of corruption going on in there, too. I could call them all out, but God would probably get mad at me, and I'd never be on this platform again. You'd look at me and go, "Brandon, what did you just say?" because there's a lot of sin in there. Seriously bad stuff. But they are held accountable for it on the other side. You think you got away with it, but you didn't. You're going to be held accountable for everything you thought you did in private. You're going to stand before the Lord one day and give an account for how you handled things. To whom much is given, much is required. I'm more scared for you than anything. Some of you are in trouble. If people really saw the stink behind the stage, they'd say, "Oh my God." Seriously, that's why Brandon just stays to himself. I don't go into all the big preacher circles. I go where the Holy Spirit leads me. He says, "Go here, don't go there. Don't go to that platform. Follow my voice, son." I asked, "What about this one?" He said, "That's where prophets go to die." No joke. He said that's where prophetic voices go to die. When He said that, I got chills. I was driving down the road. I told Diana, "This is what He just said." God said, "I'm cleaning house in 2026." First Corinthians 5:5 is going to become manifest in a great capacity for a lot of people. It's for those who touch the gold, the glory, and the girls. Judgment begins in my house first. Great TV stations will fall, and they will be no more soon. You're going to see ministers that you thought would be on platforms for the rest of their lives who will be no more. They'll be like Ananias and Sapphira. They're gone. You're going to go, "What happened there?" No explanation. They just went. Not all of them are bad; some are just getting old and need to go home. But some will. Over the next three years, you're going to see a lot of new faces coming up—the new Davids, the new Esthers—and you're going to see platforms explode seemingly out of nowhere. You're going to go, "Where in the world did they come from?" It's just the hand of the Lord using them because they said, "Here am I, Lord, send me." And you're going to see a lot of the people that were somebodies and such-and-such be nothing. It will be just like Saul walking through when the anointing was stripped away from him. So, he rises up a new David to take the helm. Will you be my new David? He says, "Will you be my new person? Here am I, Lord, send me." But if I can't handle it, and I don't want to ever cause pain to any of your people, I don't want to be what I have seen. If I don't have the character to handle the anointing, if I don't have the character to handle the platform, don't give it to me. If I'm going to hurt thousands and thousands of people in the future and I don't see it, please. Because it's like what I heard Joyce say: the bigger the platform, the bigger the level of how many people you can hurt. And I don't want to hurt people. And that's why I say, "Lord, create in me a clean heart." Help me stay right with you. Because it's staying on that vine, staying on that life source, that's going to get you there. It's whenever you separate yourself from the life source that the branch eventually dies. The branch doesn't die overnight. Just like it is if you prune a tree, it dies over time.]
